findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is always True regardless of what `Settings()` does. This test can never fail and provides zero coverage signal.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion, e.g. check a specific attribute value: `assert Settings().environment == "dev"`, or remove the test entirely if the existing `test_defaults` already covers this.

findings.message: Over-mocked test: `resolved_log_level` is patched on the very class under test, then the test asserts the mock returns `"MOCKED"`. This only confirms that `unittest.mock.patch.object` works — it cannot detect regressions in the real `resolved_log_level` implementation.
findings.suggestion: Remove the mock entirely and assert on the real behaviour, e.g. `assert Settings(environment="dev").resolved_log_level == "DEBUG"` (which is already tested in `test_resolved_log_level_is_debug_in_dev`). If the goal is to test a new code path, add a direct assertion without patching the method under test.
